properties.winery_description:
Welcome to Stoller Family Estate. Our estate offers a selection of experiences designed to make your visit an unforgettable trip to Oregon wine country.

Stoller Family Estate is a 400-acre parcel located on the southern slope of the Dundee Hills. Pioneering Oregonian and Founder Bill Stellar purchased his family’s second-generation farm in 1993 with the vision of cultivating an enduring legacy for the land and the Oregon wine industry. Over the last two decades, Bill has patiently transformed the property into the largest contiguous vineyard in the Dundee Hills. Our estate features the world’s first LEED Gold-certified winery and a net-zero tasting room with panoramic vineyard views.

Our doors are open daily from 10:30 am to 5:30 pm for tastings and wine pickup. Reservations available, walk-ins always welcome!

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

To explore our offerings or make a reservation, give our concierge team a call at (503) 864.3404. For parties of 13 or more please call our team. We are happy to talk through options and availability.

Please note: Well-behaved dogs who are kept on a leash at all times are welcome to accompany any seasonally available outdoor tasting experience, such as our patio or lawn. As we are a full food service establishment, dogs are not permitted indoors.

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
The Stoller Family Estate Team

Named for the herd that roams the nearby hills, Elk Cove Vineyards was founded in 1974 and was the first vineyard and winery in what is now the Yamhill-Carlton AVA. Tucked into the foothills of the Coast Range Mountains, the tasting room has spectacular views of the surrounding vineyards.

Second-generation winemaker Adam Campbell produces excellent Single Vineyard Pinot Noirs as well as crisp aromatic Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc and Riesling with an emphasis on estate-grown fruit. Living his whole life among the vines, Adam has tremendous respect for the land: "High-quality wine really starts in the vineyard. The time I spend stomping through our nearly 400 acres of vines not only helps us make good decisions in viticulture, it also informs our winemaking decisions."

properties.Owner3: BARRON GORDON A & BARRON JOAN L

properties.winery_description: As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

Appassionata Estate began as a passion project for the renowned German winemaker Ernst Loosen. Erni’s lifelong love for exploring the world’s great Pinot Noirs drew him to Oregon nearly three decades ago. Over many visits, collecting wines and friends along the way, he realized that something special was going on in the Willamette Valley and began to dream of making wine here himself.

Never one to merely dream, Erni launched a small, collaborative project with J. Christopher Wines in 2005, bottling a very limited, highly selected Pinot Noir that he named Appassionata. A few vintages later, he put down roots in Oregon, purchasing 40 acres in the Chehalem Mountains, planting the Appassionata Vineyard, and building the winery. His long-term vision was to establish an enduring family wine estate, dedicated to growing and producing exquisitely expressive wines with extraordinary ageability. It has been two decades in the making, but that vision is now being fully realized.

As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

Planted on the south side of Chehalem Mountains in 1976, Trout Lily Ranch is the newest label from historic Adams Vineyard focusing on estate grown pinot noir. Farmhouse tastings by appointment with charming views of the sustainably grown vineyard.

Adelsheim, located in Newberg, Ore., was founded with an optimistic spirit and a lofty dream: to create world-class Pinot noir and Chardonnay in an undiscovered wine region, the Chehalem Mountains. Since 1971, we have obsessively pursued exceptional wines of place, that celebrate the unique bounty of our estate vineyards. As a founding winery of the Willamette Valley and Chehalem Mountains first winery, Adelsheim has played an instrumental role in nearly every aspect of the Oregon wine story. As leaders, we believe it is our duty to be steadfast stewards of this abundant land and to rally our community in support of equity and education. Adelsheim is a LIVE certified sustainable winery, and a seven-time Wine & Spirits Top 100 Winery. For more information, visit our website.

As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

Since 1999, my family and team have been working towards our vision of stewarding naturally and responsibly farmed, estate vineyard terroirs that best represent the diverse appellations of our world-class Willamette Valley. Our Pinot Noirs and Chardonnays are a three-dimensional vinous tour of our beautiful state’s ancient volcanic and marine sedimentary hillsides.

All of us here at Bergström Wines are humbled by, and thankful for, the unwavering support of our community and clients. We give thanks to those who continue to make our labor of love possible by supporting our wines.

All tastings and wine pickups are held at our family home in the Dundee Hills. For information regarding reservations please email us at reservations@bergstromwines.com

Thank you once again for your support of our wines and our family and the Oregon winegrowing community!

Josh Bergström

Adelsheim, located in Newberg, Ore., was founded with an optimistic spirit and a lofty dream: to create world-class Pinot noir and Chardonnay in an undiscovered wine region, the Chehalem Mountains. Since 1971, we have obsessively pursued exceptional wines of place, that celebrate the unique bounty of our estate vineyards. As a founding winery of the Willamette Valley and Chehalem Mountains first winery, Adelsheim has played an instrumental role in nearly every aspect of the Oregon wine story. As leaders, we believe it is our duty to be steadfast stewards of this abundant land and to rally our community in support of equity and education. Adelsheim is a LIVE certified sustainable winery, and a seven-time Wine & Spirits Top 100 Winery. For more information, visit our website.

Our winery is located in Newberg, Oregon, just a vineyard stroll up the hill from Oregon’s renowned Allison Inn and Spa.

Our tasting room is a 1926 farmhouse that was elegantly and meticulously remodeled in 2012. Our extensive patio, with a gas fire pit, overlooks our exquisite gardens, fountains, ponds and vineyards.

While Pinot Noir is our main focus, we also produce a Gamay Noir, and an array of crowd-pleasing whites, including Pinot Noir Rosé, Chardonnay, an off-dry Riesling and even a dry Gewürztraminer. Our five-six wine flight is $35, and the fee is waived with a three-bottle purchase and complimentary for members and their guests.

Tastings are held Thursday-Monday from 11AM to 5PM. 

Reservations are appreciated, but walk-ins are always welcome!

Please respect our policy of no pets or outside food. Thank you!

Le Cadeau Pinot noir is the result of meticulous winegrowing and winemaking practices. Our vineyard is located on a high-elevation volcanic basalt hillside, and is often described as the rockiest vineyard in Oregon. From this extraordinary place comes distinctive Oregon Pinot noir. Our tasting room is conveniently located in downtown Dundee just off Hwy 99W at The Dundee Hotel.

Natalie's Estate Winery has been committed to excellence since 1999 and is a family-owned boutique winery dedicated to handcrafting small quantities of ultra-premium wines. 

We have a large selection of varietals such as Chardonnay, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Sangiovese, Meritage, Cabernet Franc, Rose of Pinot Noir, Malbec, Petite Verdot, Syrah, Zinfandel, Pinot Gris, Pinot Noir, and other rich &robust red blends. Our wines have intense varietal character, showing distinct personalities reflective of their terroir using traditional European winemaking techniques. 

We offer Elevated Tasting Experiences where you can choose personalized tours and tastings with our owner/winemaker including Barrel & Wine Tasting with our Winemaker; Vineyard Tour and Wine Tasting, Private Blending Class, and a Private Tasting. For groups or teams, we offer a Winemaker reception for up to 35 guests and also a sumptuous Four-Course Vineyard Cuisine Dinner at the winery. 

Our vineyards and varietals represent their distinctly different terroir. These superb wines are built to age gracefully with complex characteristics distinctive of where they were grown. 

We select fruit from the finest Pacific Northwest vineyards as well as our Willamette Valley Pinot Noir Estate Vineyard. The owners, Boyd and Cassandra Teegarden are involved in every step of the way including hosting you in the welcoming tasting room! 

Natalie's Estate Winery is nestled in the quiet foothills of the Chehalem Mountain AVA in Oregon's Willamette Valley. 

We are proud partners with Tour Devine by Heli to host guests coming by helicopter to our property. 

Come visit and enjoy the wines and setting! We are open by appointment only. Call us at 503-807-5008 to make a reservation.

properties.winery_description:
Visit us on Ribbon Ridge to enjoy expressive wines and artwork crafted with intention by our artist and winemaker James Frey.

At Trisaetum Winery, we specialize in Pinot Noir, Riesling, Chardonnay and Sparkling wines. Daily, we offer a selection of red and white flights that you can enjoy outside on our covered patio or in our art gallery tasting room, where the creative intertwinement between art and wine is always on display. Or, for a uniquely immersive tasting experience, join us for a private tasting in our barrel cave.

We are also home to Brixeur Spirits, which was started by the family at Trisaetum in 2020. With vodka distilled from estate grapes, gins with locally foraged botanicals, and whiskeys aged in wine casks, our approach to spirits is heavily influenced by our winemaking roots. Spirits tastings are offered in the cottage on weekends, and in the wine tasting room daily.

Since 1999, my family and team have been working towards our vision of stewarding naturally and responsibly farmed, estate vineyard terroirs that best represent the diverse appellations of our world-class Willamette Valley. Our Pinot Noirs and Chardonnays are a three-dimensional vinous tour of our beautiful state’s ancient volcanic and marine sedimentary hillsides.

All of us here at Bergström Wines are humbled by, and thankful for, the unwavering support of our community and clients. We give thanks to those who continue to make our labor of love possible by supporting our wines.

All tastings and wine pickups are held at our family home in the Dundee Hills. For information regarding reservations please email us at reservations@bergstromwines.com

Thank you once again for your support of our wines and our family and the Oregon winegrowing community!

Josh Bergström

All tastings and wine pickups are held at our family home in the Dundee Hills. For information regarding reservations please email us at reservations@bergstromwines.com

Thank you once again for your support of our wines and our family and the Oregon winegrowing community!

Josh Bergström

Utopia is a grower-producer of premium Pinot Noir, Chardonnay and other varietals. The winery has been consistent over the last 20 years in its brand approach, which is to avoid all pretense and to be an approachable safe haven for the wine interested. Does it sound like a visit to Utopia could be added to your travel itinerary? Special Note: Dans definition of Utopia is The perfect place, and no place. If you visit, you will understand why. And a fair warning about Dans wines. When you taste them, you may experience Bliss, Paradise and Eden!Cheers!The Utopia Team

Since its inception in 1993, Archery Summit has been devoted to crafting exceptional Pinot Noir from its more than 80 acres across five estate vineyards. Our philosophy is driven by a commitment to understanding the intricacies and unique personalities of each of our estate vineyards, allowing us to craft wines that vividly express their distinct places of origin. Over the past twenty-five years we have developed an international reputation for producing wines among the finest and most distinct of New World Pinot Noir. Our tasting room is open daily from 10am to 4pm by appointment. For more information on tastings and to reserve an experience, visit https://www.archerysummit.com/visit/ . We look forward to hosting you during your visit.

Adelsheim, located in Newberg, Ore., was founded with an optimistic spirit and a lofty dream: to create world-class Pinot noir and Chardonnay in an undiscovered wine region, the Chehalem Mountains. Since 1971, we have obsessively pursued exceptional wines of place, that celebrate the unique bounty of our estate vineyards. As a founding winery of the Willamette Valley and Chehalem Mountains first winery, Adelsheim has played an instrumental role in nearly every aspect of the Oregon wine story. As leaders, we believe it is our duty to be steadfast stewards of this abundant land and to rally our community in support of equity and education. Adelsheim is a LIVE certified sustainable winery, and a seven-time Wine & Spirits Top 100 Winery. For more information, visit our website.

Welcome to Stoller Family Estate. Our estate offers a selection of experiences designed to make your visit an unforgettable trip to Oregon wine country.

Stoller Family Estate is a 400-acre parcel located on the southern slope of the Dundee Hills. Pioneering Oregonian and Founder Bill Stellar purchased his family’s second-generation farm in 1993 with the vision of cultivating an enduring legacy for the land and the Oregon wine industry. Over the last two decades, Bill has patiently transformed the property into the largest contiguous vineyard in the Dundee Hills. Our estate features the world’s first LEED Gold-certified winery and a net-zero tasting room with panoramic vineyard views.

Our doors are open daily from 10:30 am to 5:30 pm for tastings and wine pickup. Reservations available, walk-ins always welcome!

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

To explore our offerings or make a reservation, give our concierge team a call at (503) 864.3404. For parties of 13 or more please call our team. We are happy to talk through options and availability.

Please note: Well-behaved dogs who are kept on a leash at all times are welcome to accompany any seasonally available outdoor tasting experience, such as our patio or lawn. As we are a full food service establishment, dogs are not permitted indoors.

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
The Stoller Family Estate Team

As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

Adelsheim, located in Newberg, Ore., was founded with an optimistic spirit and a lofty dream: to create world-class Pinot noir and Chardonnay in an undiscovered wine region, the Chehalem Mountains. Since 1971, we have obsessively pursued exceptional wines of place, that celebrate the unique bounty of our estate vineyards. As a founding winery of the Willamette Valley and Chehalem Mountains first winery, Adelsheim has played an instrumental role in nearly every aspect of the Oregon wine story. As leaders, we believe it is our duty to be steadfast stewards of this abundant land and to rally our community in support of equity and education. Adelsheim is a LIVE certified sustainable winery, and a seven-time Wine & Spirits Top 100 Winery. For more information, visit our website.

In 1987, Cooper Mountain Vineyards began a quest that has brought us to more than 100 acres, a commitment to qualitywinesand a welcomingtasting room. Today Cooper Mountain offers certified organic and biodynamic Pinot noir, Pinot gris, and Chardonnay wines. We invite you to come and be a part of our story.

Since 2013, Flâneur Wines has crafted exceptional wines from their two estate vineyards in the Willamette Valley: La Belle Promenade (Chehalem Mountains AVA) and Flânerie Vineyard (Ribbon Ridge AVA). Winemaker Anthony Sereni creates an award-winning portfolio of Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Gamay, Sparkling Wine and more, all organically dry farmed in-house. 

The name "Flâneur" captures the spirit of leisurely wanderers in 19th-century Paris, reflecting the idea that slowing down and embracing curiosity leads to a life of significance. This philosophy, inspired by Founder Marty Doerschlag, is embodied not only in the wines but also in the hospitality.

Flâneur Wines Tasting Room is housed in a century old Grain Elevator located downtown Carlton. Guests are met with warm hospitality, curated tastings, light bites and tours of the thoughtfully restored building. Once the heart of rural agriculture in the Willamette Valley is now the beating heart of fine wines, beautiful architecture and thoughtful hospitality.

Since 1999, my family and team have been working towards our vision of stewarding naturally and responsibly farmed, estate vineyard terroirs that best represent the diverse appellations of our world-class Willamette Valley. Our Pinot Noirs and Chardonnays are a three-dimensional vinous tour of our beautiful state’s ancient volcanic and marine sedimentary hillsides.

All of us here at Bergström Wines are humbled by, and thankful for, the unwavering support of our community and clients. We give thanks to those who continue to make our labor of love possible by supporting our wines.

All tastings and wine pickups are held at our family home in the Dundee Hills. For information regarding reservations please email us at reservations@bergstromwines.com

Thank you once again for your support of our wines and our family and the Oregon winegrowing community!

Josh Bergström

As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

By appointment. Two locations - The Villa at the Estate Vineyard in Newberg & Winery in Tualatin (Fall - Spring). Each offers a different experience from a picnic or seated tasting flight while you enjoy the charm and serene views of the Willamette Valley at the vineyard to the rustic urban winery environment for tastings or events. Private tasting experiences available with special tasting flights, vineyard walk/tour of our Biodynamic farming practices and chef-prepared lunches for private groups up to 20. Founded in 1998, the Lachini Family believes that the vineyard is the wine and nurtures their 45-acre vineyard's distinctive terroir and producing world class Pinot noirs of prodigious quality with the goal for generations to come. Meticulous management of each vine is through biodynamic/organic farming practices, while they combine state of the art winemaking and old-world technique to handcraft wines of complexity and grace- each one, believed, a reflection of its soul and unique place. You are invited to delight your senses through the lens of a glass. 

SOUL, PASSION, INTENSE, ADVENTUROUS, SUSTAINABLE, CONNECT, BALANCE, SINGULAR, QUALITY, HEALTH, LAUGHTER, HEARTACHE, PERSISTENCE, FAMILY & FRIENDS, GRATEFUL, GOOD TIMES, CELEBRATE! Salute'! Ron & Marianne Lachini

Welcome to Stoller Family Estate. Our estate offers a selection of experiences designed to make your visit an unforgettable trip to Oregon wine country.

Stoller Family Estate is a 400-acre parcel located on the southern slope of the Dundee Hills. Pioneering Oregonian and Founder Bill Stellar purchased his family’s second-generation farm in 1993 with the vision of cultivating an enduring legacy for the land and the Oregon wine industry. Over the last two decades, Bill has patiently transformed the property into the largest contiguous vineyard in the Dundee Hills. Our estate features the world’s first LEED Gold-certified winery and a net-zero tasting room with panoramic vineyard views.

Our doors are open daily from 10:30 am to 5:30 pm for tastings and wine pickup. Reservations available, walk-ins always welcome!

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

To explore our offerings or make a reservation, give our concierge team a call at (503) 864.3404. For parties of 13 or more please call our team. We are happy to talk through options and availability.

Please note: Well-behaved dogs who are kept on a leash at all times are welcome to accompany any seasonally available outdoor tasting experience, such as our patio or lawn. As we are a full food service establishment, dogs are not permitted indoors.

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
The Stoller Family Estate Team

properties.winery_description:
Benza Vineyards, founded by Ron Benza and Trish Damkroger, presents a sanctuary for wine enthusiasts to experience the elegant, complex flavors of the region meticulously crafted by winemaker Paden West. Nestled amidst tranquil surroundings with picturesque vineyard views and an idyllic farm setting with wraparound porches, guests are invited to indulge in Benza’s wines from the Laurelwood soil while enjoying the company of friendly dogs and chickens, and personalized hospitality, making it the perfect day escape just 25 minutes from Portland.


CCOF Organic Certified
LIVE Certified Sustainable Grapes
Salmon Safe

Alloro Vineyard is 110 acres of uniquely contoured, loess-derived, Laurelwood soil. Located on a southwest-facing slope in Oregon's Chehalem Mountains, this special place is the heart and soul of our wines and is personally cared for by founder David Nemarnik. We are a limited production producer of 100% Estate wines, crafting all our wines from our sustainably farmed single vineyard site, which also hosts a diverse mixture of many other crops, plants, and animals forming an extraordinary whole farm setting. Our UC Davis educated, Burgundy trained winemaker, Tom Fitzpatrick, believes that a wine should be an "expression of place, at a moment in time" and should therefore capture the unique personality of a site as it is expressed in each vintage. Terroir-driven wines that reflect this philosophy are our focus.

Eric Lemelson planted 3/4 of an acre of Pinot noir on a hillside farm east of Newberg in 1995. He enjoyed viticulture so much that within three years he planted more than 30 additional acres near Carlton and began to design the present day winery. Today, Lemelson Vineyards owns and manages over 124 acres at five sites spanning three AVAs. Each of the five estate vineyards have been farmed organically since the time their vines were first planted. Notably, all five vineyards have been certified organic by Oregon Tilth each year since October of 2004. The gravity-flow winery reflects a strong commitment to sustainability and a no compromises approach to quality winemaking.

properties.winery_description:
Named for the herd that roams the nearby hills, Elk Cove Vineyards was founded in 1974 and was the first vineyard and winery in what is now the Yamhill-Carlton AVA. Tucked into the foothills of the Coast Range Mountains, the tasting room has spectacular views of the surrounding vineyards.

Second-generation winemaker Adam Campbell produces excellent Single Vineyard Pinot Noirs as well as crisp aromatic Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc and Riesling with an emphasis on estate-grown fruit. Living his whole life among the vines, Adam has tremendous respect for the land: "High-quality wine really starts in the vineyard. The time I spend stomping through our nearly 400 acres of vines not only helps us make good decisions in viticulture, it also informs our winemaking decisions."

properties.Owner3: KNIGHT BRUCE R & KNIGHT JOHANNA M

properties.winery_description: Adelsheim, located in Newberg, Ore., was founded with an optimistic spirit and a lofty dream: to create world-class Pinot noir and Chardonnay in an undiscovered wine region, the Chehalem Mountains. Since 1971, we have obsessively pursued exceptional wines of place, that celebrate the unique bounty of our estate vineyards. As a founding winery of the Willamette Valley and Chehalem Mountains first winery, Adelsheim has played an instrumental role in nearly every aspect of the Oregon wine story. As leaders, we believe it is our duty to be steadfast stewards of this abundant land and to rally our community in support of equity and education. Adelsheim is a LIVE certified sustainable winery, and a seven-time Wine & Spirits Top 100 Winery. For more information, visit our website.

Since 1999, my family and team have been working towards our vision of stewarding naturally and responsibly farmed, estate vineyard terroirs that best represent the diverse appellations of our world-class Willamette Valley. Our Pinot Noirs and Chardonnays are a three-dimensional vinous tour of our beautiful state’s ancient volcanic and marine sedimentary hillsides.

All of us here at Bergström Wines are humbled by, and thankful for, the unwavering support of our community and clients. We give thanks to those who continue to make our labor of love possible by supporting our wines.

All tastings and wine pickups are held at our family home in the Dundee Hills. For information regarding reservations please email us at reservations@bergstromwines.com

Thank you once again for your support of our wines and our family and the Oregon winegrowing community!

Josh Bergström

Utopia is a grower-producer of premium Pinot Noir, Chardonnay and other varietals. The winery has been consistent over the last 20 years in its brand approach, which is to avoid all pretense and to be an approachable safe haven for the wine interested. Does it sound like a visit to Utopia could be added to your travel itinerary? Special Note: Dans definition of Utopia is The perfect place, and no place. If you visit, you will understand why. And a fair warning about Dans wines. When you taste them, you may experience Bliss, Paradise and Eden!Cheers!The Utopia Team

— Colene Clemens Vineyards —
Colene Clemens was founded in 2005 by Joe and Victoria Stark with the express purpose of making exceptional Pinot noir. The property is named in honor of Victoria’s mother, Colene Clemens. Wine production began in 2008, and Winemaker & Vineyard Manager, Stephen Goff has been dedicated to the development and planting of the vineyard as well as the production of our stunning wines since the very beginning.

— The Estate —
Spanning 122 acres where the Chehalem Mountains converge with Ribbon Ridge, the estate contains both sedimentary soils from the Wellsdale & Hazelaire series, and volcanic soils from the Witzel series. Elevation climbs from 350 to 650 feet, where the winery perches with sweeping views of the coast range and valley. Currently 62 acres are planted to Pinot noir & Chardonnay.

— Visiting the Winery —
Our winery and patio are open for tasting by appointment. It's easy to plan your visit! To make your appointment, you can book online at www.coleneclemens.com/visit, give us a call at 503-662-4687, or email us at info@coleneclemens.com to secure your reservation prior to visiting. We look forward to hosting you!

For over five decades, Ponzi Vineyards has been guided by a deep respect for the land, crafting wines that embody the quiet beauty and untamed spirit of the Willamette Valley. Nestled in the heart of the Laurelwood District AVA, our estate vineyards are home to some of Oregon’s oldest own-rooted vines, thriving in the energetically-rich Laurelwood soils that define our region.

Sustainability has always been our promise, evolving and improving each year to ensure long-term care of the land we farm. In the cellar, a philosophy of low-intervention winemaking—gravity-flow methods, native yeast fermentations, and careful handling—reveals the true essence of each vintage. Every bottle is a pure reflection of place, shaped by time, soil, and an enduring commitment to harmony.

Open daily for tastings 11am to 4pm, last seating at 3:30pm. Reservations recommended; walk-ins are welcome and subject to availability.

Alloro Vineyard is 110 acres of uniquely contoured, loess-derived, Laurelwood soil. Located on a southwest-facing slope in Oregon's Chehalem Mountains, this special place is the heart and soul of our wines and is personally cared for by founder David Nemarnik. We are a limited production producer of 100% Estate wines, crafting all our wines from our sustainably farmed single vineyard site, which also hosts a diverse mixture of many other crops, plants, and animals forming an extraordinary whole farm setting. Our UC Davis educated, Burgundy trained winemaker, Tom Fitzpatrick, believes that a wine should be an "expression of place, at a moment in time" and should therefore capture the unique personality of a site as it is expressed in each vintage. Terroir-driven wines that reflect this philosophy are our focus.

properties.Owner3: SHULL SCOTT EVAN & SHULL ANN LOUISE
properties.winery_description:
Perched on a ridge in Oregon’s Laurelwood District AVA, Raptor Ridge Winery is a sanctuary for native birds of prey — and for guests seeking a peaceful, vine-side tasting experience. Open year-round, our tasting room offers sweeping views of four Cascade Range mountaintops and a tranquil escape from the everyday. Guests can enjoy curated tasting flights, a shorter abridged flight, or wines by the glass and bottle — all served alongside warm hospitality and stunning vineyard views.

We craft our wines from sustainably farmed vineyards, including our LIVE-Certified estate, Tuscowallame Vineyard. In addition to classic Pinot Noir and Pinot Gris, we produce distinctive varietals such as Auxerrois, estate-grown Grüner Veltliner, and traditional method sparkling wines.

Tasting appointments may be made by clicking Book Now above, by calling us at 503-628-8463, or emailing info@RaptorRidgeWinery.com.

Hazelfern started their wine craft in 2006 while making five gallons of Pinot Noir in a dimly lit Portland, Oregon basement on the former (circa 1895) site of Hazel Fern Farms. In 2014, a quarter-life crisis pushed Bryan and Laura Laing to pack up their basket press, two daughters, and a ping pong table for life on a wine farm in the Chehalem Mountains of Newberg, Oregon.

Today, Hazelfern remains a passionate husband and wife winemaking team focused on producing delicious wines and providing an exceptional and unique Oregon wine tasting experience in the Hazelfern wine barn.

Hazelfern wines are best paired with special good people, great stories, loud music, and Tuesday night farm dinners!

Hope to see you soon on the farm!

Welcome to Stoller Family Estate. Our estate offers a selection of experiences designed to make your visit an unforgettable trip to Oregon wine country.

Stoller Family Estate is a 400-acre parcel located on the southern slope of the Dundee Hills. Pioneering Oregonian and Founder Bill Stellar purchased his family’s second-generation farm in 1993 with the vision of cultivating an enduring legacy for the land and the Oregon wine industry. Over the last two decades, Bill has patiently transformed the property into the largest contiguous vineyard in the Dundee Hills. Our estate features the world’s first LEED Gold-certified winery and a net-zero tasting room with panoramic vineyard views.

Our doors are open daily from 10:30 am to 5:30 pm for tastings and wine pickup. Reservations available, walk-ins always welcome!

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

To explore our offerings or make a reservation, give our concierge team a call at (503) 864.3404. For parties of 13 or more please call our team. We are happy to talk through options and availability.

Please note: Well-behaved dogs who are kept on a leash at all times are welcome to accompany any seasonally available outdoor tasting experience, such as our patio or lawn. As we are a full food service establishment, dogs are not permitted indoors.

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
The Stoller Family Estate Team

properties.winery_description: Le Cadeau Pinot noir is the result of meticulous winegrowing and winemaking practices. Our vineyard is located on a high-elevation volcanic basalt hillside, and is often described as the rockiest vineyard in Oregon. From this extraordinary place comes distinctive Oregon Pinot noir. Our tasting room is conveniently located in downtown Dundee just off Hwy 99W at The Dundee Hotel.

properties.Owner3: HOUSE JOHN S & HOUSE WENDY K

properties.winery_description:
For over five decades, Ponzi Vineyards has been guided by a deep respect for the land, crafting wines that embody the quiet beauty and untamed spirit of the Willamette Valley. Nestled in the heart of the Laurelwood District AVA, our estate vineyards are home to some of Oregon’s oldest own-rooted vines, thriving in the energetically-rich Laurelwood soils that define our region.

Sustainability has always been our promise, evolving and improving each year to ensure long-term care of the land we farm. In the cellar, a philosophy of low-intervention winemaking—gravity-flow methods, native yeast fermentations, and careful handling—reveals the true essence of each vintage. Every bottle is a pure reflection of place, shaped by time, soil, and an enduring commitment to harmony.

Open daily for tastings 11am to 4pm, last seating at 3:30pm. Reservations recommended; walk-ins are welcome and subject to availability.

In 1987, Cooper Mountain Vineyards began a quest that has brought us to more than 100 acres, a commitment to qualitywinesand a welcomingtasting room. Today Cooper Mountain offers certified organic and biodynamic Pinot noir, Pinot gris, and Chardonnay wines. We invite you to come and be a part of our story.

properties.winery_description: Welcome to Gemini Vineyards, a family-farmed estate situated on Laurelwood soil at 500ft elevation in the Chehalem Mountains. Gemini Vineyards was established in 1995. Our commitment to regenerative and sustainable farming on our site ensures that our wines are produced to display the characters of the terroir and the love of land and wine. For those looking to experience an authentic vineyard experience while sipping exceptional wine from the Chehalem Mountains, you'll find a welcomed spot at our table, right among the vines in a cozy, covered gazebo. You might just be greeted by our resident flock of sheep and chickens as you experience an intimate wine tasting and vineyard tour. Whether new to wine, or an active wine enthusiast, Gemini is unpretentious, with the strong belief that wine is for everyone. We focus on producing expressive Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Gamay Noir, Pinot Gris, and Pinot Noir Rose. To honor our 30 years of vines and wine, Gemini Vineyards will be opening a tasting room across from the estate in Sherwood in late 2025. Please join us.

Whether new to wine, or an active wine enthusiast, Gemini is unpretentious, with the strong belief that wine is for everyone. We focus on producing expressive Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Gamay Noir, Pinot Gris, and Pinot Noir Rose. To honor our 30 years of vines and wine, Gemini Vineyards will be opening a tasting room across from the estate in Sherwood in late 2025. Please join us.

properties.winery_description:
Welcome to Stoller Family Estate. Our estate offers a selection of experiences designed to make your visit an unforgettable trip to Oregon wine country.

Stoller Family Estate is a 400-acre parcel located on the southern slope of the Dundee Hills. Pioneering Oregonian and Founder Bill Stellar purchased his family’s second-generation farm in 1993 with the vision of cultivating an enduring legacy for the land and the Oregon wine industry. Over the last two decades, Bill has patiently transformed the property into the largest contiguous vineyard in the Dundee Hills. Our estate features the world’s first LEED Gold-certified winery and a net-zero tasting room with panoramic vineyard views.

Our doors are open daily from 10:30 am to 5:30 pm for tastings and wine pickup. Reservations available, walk-ins always welcome!

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

To explore our offerings or make a reservation, give our concierge team a call at (503) 864.3404. For parties of 13 or more please call our team. We are happy to talk through options and availability.

Please note: Well-behaved dogs who are kept on a leash at all times are welcome to accompany any seasonally available outdoor tasting experience, such as our patio or lawn. As we are a full food service establishment, dogs are not permitted indoors.

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
The Stoller Family Estate Team

As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

properties.Owner3: KENNEDY SEAN MICHAEL & GARTH CASEY JORDAN
KENNEDY SEAN MICHAEL & GARTH CASEY JORDAN
KENNEDY SEAN MICHAEL & GARTH CASEY JORDAN

properties.winery_description:
It was all but inevitable that we'd make our living from the earth. Cody Wright, our founder and winemaker, was raised by esteemed pioneers of the Oregon wine industry. He spent childhood days wandering between vineyard rows and moving fermenters with forklifts. And his wife, Marque Wright, who assists in managing the winery business, grew up in southern Oregon amidst timber operations and cattle farms. Together, they are the soul of Purple Hands. Cody's official foray into winemaking took off after he completed a degree in environmental science at the University of Oregon. He worked in various vineyard management and winemaking roles at Ken Wright Cellars, and ROCO. Abroad, he further honed his craft at Knappstein Winery in Claire Valley, South Australia and Ata Rangi in Martinborough, New Zealand. In 2005, Cody established Purple Hands as a small boutique winery. In ten years time he and his wife Marque, transformed the winery into a cult favorite that produces several thousand cases a year of critically acclaimed wine. 

Our winery's name speaks to the earthen processes and exquisite products of winemaking. The toils and stains of working vineyards and crushing grapes that lead to elevated experience's combining flavors of food and wine. We hope you'll share our passion with friends and family and be uplifted by Oregons precise and pure expressions!

Privé Vineyard is a boutique winery crafting high-quality, estate grown Pinot Noirs. Our old-vine vineyard was planted in 1980 and remains own-rooted until this day. Our winemaking philosophy is to embrace the incredible Oregon terroir with a traditional approach. We produce less than 1000 cases of our Pinot Noirs, Chardonnay, Syrah and Ruby Port. Please visit our website for more information or to book an appointment.

Our winery is located in Newberg, Oregon, just a vineyard stroll up the hill from Oregon’s renowned Allison Inn and Spa.

Our tasting room is a 1926 farmhouse that was elegantly and meticulously remodeled in 2012. Our extensive patio, with a gas fire pit, overlooks our exquisite gardens, fountains, ponds and vineyards.

While Pinot Noir is our main focus, we also produce a Gamay Noir, and an array of crowd-pleasing whites, including Pinot Noir Rosé, Chardonnay, an off-dry Riesling and even a dry Gewürztraminer. Our five-six wine flight is $35, and the fee is waived with a three-bottle purchase and complimentary for members and their guests.

Tastings are held Thursday-Monday from 11AM to 5PM. 

Reservations are appreciated, but walk-ins are always welcome!

Please respect our policy of no pets or outside food. Thank you!

In 1987, Cooper Mountain Vineyards began a quest that has brought us to more than 100 acres, a commitment to qualitywinesand a welcomingtasting room. Today Cooper Mountain offers certified organic and biodynamic Pinot noir, Pinot gris, and Chardonnay wines. We invite you to come and be a part of our story.

In 1987, Cooper Mountain Vineyards began a quest that has brought us to more than 100 acres, a commitment to qualitywinesand a welcomingtasting room. Today Cooper Mountain offers certified organic and biodynamic Pinot noir, Pinot gris, and Chardonnay wines. We invite you to come and be a part of our story.

Eric Lemelson planted 3/4 of an acre of Pinot noir on a hillside farm east of Newberg in 1995. He enjoyed viticulture so much that within three years he planted more than 30 additional acres near Carlton and began to design the present day winery. Today, Lemelson Vineyards owns and manages over 124 acres at five sites spanning three AVAs. Each of the five estate vineyards have been farmed organically since the time their vines were first planted. Notably, all five vineyards have been certified organic by Oregon Tilth each year since October of 2004. The gravity-flow winery reflects a strong commitment to sustainability and a no compromises approach to quality winemaking.

Come experience wine country like never before, with a taste of Burgundy in the heart of the Willamette Valley. Founded in 1989 by Ken and Grace Evenstad, Domaine Serene set out to craft world-class Pinot Noir. After three decades, we are proud to share our award-winning sparkling wine, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from both our Oregon and Burgundy estate vineyards. Our tasting room offers a range of experiences for every guest. Whether you’re enjoying a glass or bottle of wine, or embarking on a more immersive journey, we have something for all. We have Sommelier-led flights that dive deep into our rich story, or opt for a self-guided tasting with a curated food pairing. Walk-ins are always welcome, or you can make a reservation for more expanded experiences. Whether you wish to relax or learn, Domaine Serene is your premier destination for creating unique, unforgettable moments.
 
To explore our experiences or book online, visit: www.domaineserene.com/visit-us
Or contact us at (503) 864-4600 or hospitality@domaineserene.com

At Sokol Blosser Winery, we offer a multitude of options to help you plan your perfect wine tasting experience. Although we recommend making a reservation online or giving us a call to reserve your table, we do accommodate walk-ins. Our seated tastings allow us to customize your tasting experience and add on culinary options from butcher's boards with a selection of meats and cheeses to culinary delights from our estate chef. 
 
Farm & Forage, our acclaimed food and wine pairing experience, is held Friday through Sunday at 1pm and requires a reservation. We host a Happy Hour in our Wine Bar from 5-7pm each Friday with a different theme every week. Every Saturday we offer a tour and wine tasting experience 10:30am. We’ll be firing up our pizza oven Memorial Weekend and running it seven days a week through the summer. Starting in June, you can join us for music and Frosé every Thursday for Live on the Lawn. Keep an eye out for Sunday vineyard hikes starting in July. 
 
More than 50 years ago, the Sokol Blosser family began growing grapes in the Dundee Hills of Oregon's Willamette Valley. Situated on a certified organic 88-acre vineyard in the Dundee Hills, with a new second 22-acre vineyard called Kalita in the Yamhill-Carlton AVA, and farming another 15 acres of vineyards in the Eola-Amity Hills, B Corp-certified Sokol Blosser Winery consistently captures the terroir of the region as expressed through the brilliance of its Estate fruit. One of the pioneering wineries of Oregon, Sokol Blosser is run by the Sokol Blosser family, who emphasize sustainability and good to the earth policies in every phase of the business.

Estate grown wines, Spectacular views, Genuine hospitality. All of our wines come from our vineyards growing adjacent to the winery. Our vineyards were established over 25 years ago, but our roots in this land go back even farther. Seated tastings on our covered patio allow you to take in the views while appreciating the wines. Our experienced staff provide an exceptional experience, and we love to answer your questions about our wines or the region. Winter's Hill Estate is located in the heart of the Dundee Hills and includes extensive natural areas of forest and meadows, as well as a Native Oak Savannah restoration site and birding trail.

Winter's Hill Estate is located in the heart of the Dundee Hills and includes extensive natural areas of forest and meadows, as well as a Native Oak Savannah restoration site and birding trail.

Come experience wine country like never before, with a taste of Burgundy in the heart of the Willamette Valley. Founded in 1989 by Ken and Grace Evenstad, Domaine Serene set out to craft world-class Pinot Noir. After three decades, we are proud to share our award-winning sparkling wine, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from both our Oregon and Burgundy estate vineyards. Our tasting room offers a range of experiences for every guest. Whether you’re enjoying a glass or bottle of wine, or embarking on a more immersive journey, we have something for all. We have Sommelier-led flights that dive deep into our rich story, or opt for a self-guided tasting with a curated food pairing. Walk-ins are always welcome, or you can make a reservation for more expanded experiences. Whether you wish to relax or learn, Domaine Serene is your premier destination for creating unique, unforgettable moments.
 
To explore our experiences or book online, visit: www.domaineserene.com/visit-us
Or contact us at (503) 864-4600 or hospitality@domaineserene.com

Welcome to Stoller Family Estate. Our estate offers a selection of experiences designed to make your visit an unforgettable trip to Oregon wine country.

Stoller Family Estate is a 400-acre parcel located on the southern slope of the Dundee Hills. Pioneering Oregonian and Founder Bill Stellar purchased his family’s second-generation farm in 1993 with the vision of cultivating an enduring legacy for the land and the Oregon wine industry. Over the last two decades, Bill has patiently transformed the property into the largest contiguous vineyard in the Dundee Hills. Our estate features the world’s first LEED Gold-certified winery and a net-zero tasting room with panoramic vineyard views.

Our doors are open daily from 10:30 am to 5:30 pm for tastings and wine pickup. Reservations available, walk-ins always welcome!

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

To explore our offerings or make a reservation, give our concierge team a call at (503) 864.3404. For parties of 13 or more please call our team. We are happy to talk through options and availability.

Please note: Well-behaved dogs who are kept on a leash at all times are welcome to accompany any seasonally available outdoor tasting experience, such as our patio or lawn. As we are a full food service establishment, dogs are not permitted indoors.

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
The Stoller Family Estate Team

Our estate features the world’s first LEED Gold-certified winery and a net-zero tasting room with panoramic vineyard views.

Our doors are open daily from 10:30 am to 5:30 pm for tastings and wine pickup. Reservations available, walk-ins always welcome!

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

To explore our offerings or make a reservation, give our concierge team a call at (503) 864.3404. For parties of 13 or more please call our team. We are happy to talk through options and availability.

Please note: Well-behaved dogs who are kept on a leash at all times are welcome to accompany any seasonally available outdoor tasting experience, such as our patio or lawn. As we are a full food service establishment, dogs are not permitted indoors.

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
The Stoller Family Estate Team

Come experience wine country like never before, with a taste of Burgundy in the heart of the Willamette Valley. Founded in 1989 by Ken and Grace Evenstad, Domaine Serene set out to craft world-class Pinot Noir. After three decades, we are proud to share our award-winning sparkling wine, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from both our Oregon and Burgundy estate vineyards. Our tasting room offers a range of experiences for every guest. Whether you’re enjoying a glass or bottle of wine, or embarking on a more immersive journey, we have something for all. We have Sommelier-led flights that dive deep into our rich story, or opt for a self-guided tasting with a curated food pairing. Walk-ins are always welcome, or you can make a reservation for more expanded experiences. Whether you wish to relax or learn, Domaine Serene is your premier destination for creating unique, unforgettable moments.
 
To explore our experiences or book online, visit: www.domaineserene.com/visit-us
Or contact us at (503) 864-4600 or hospitality@domaineserene.com

Experience Durant Vineyard’s estate-grown and crafted wines from our beautiful Dundee Hills Wine Tasting Room at Red Ridge Farms. Perched above the dramatic Willamette Valley, the Durant Vineyards Tasting Room features incredible views of Mt. Hood, Mt. Jefferson, and miles of diverse agricultural land. Enjoy seasonal bites from our talented Culinary Directors, expertly crafted to pair with our wines. Whether you’re a wine enthusiast or a casual taster, we provide a welcoming atmosphere to immerse yourself in the beauty of our surroundings and the quality of our wines. 

Reservations are recommended but not required. To make a reservation, please call 503.864.2000 Ext. 1 or visit www.durantoregon.com. We look forward to welcoming you!

White Rose Estate aims to match our winemaking goals with the emotional connections you seek in your wine experience. Since our inception in 2001, we have discovered that people value how they feel when they drink wine more than the traditional emphasis on provenance. Over the past two decades, we have focused on understanding these connections and creating wines that fit each emotional experience.

First, we identified the most prominent ways people connect emotionally with wine: social experiences, learning, collecting, pairing wine with food, and the subjective beauty that can be found in wine.

Next, we studied the characteristics in a wine that best facilitate these experiences. For example, a collectable wine must age well over time while an educational wine must teach you about the winemaking process.

With this knowledge we define our winemaking practices, selecting the appropriate vineyards, winemaking techniques, and barrel aging regime to achieve our desired objectives. Each of these steps help us to craft wines from the ground up that match the emotional connection you seek through wine.

White Rose Estate is excited to share these emotional connections with you, challenging the traditional, sense of place based mindset that is seemingly ubiquitous within wine.

Established in 1987, Domaine Drouhin Oregon is owned by the Drouhin Family of Burgundy. Fourth-generation winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin produces wines of distinction, balance, elegance, and longevity. The family’s principled approach to viticulture and winemaking and long experience with Pinot Noir and Chardonnay have earned an international reputation for excellence.

We are honored to invite you to experience wines made with French Soul from Oregon Soil. Our tasting room is open for indoor & outdoor tastings by appointment only from 10am to 3pm, daily. Please call us on (503)864-2700 with any questions.

Estate grown wines, Spectacular views, Genuine hospitality. All of our wines come from our vineyards growing adjacent to the winery. Our vineyards were established over 25 years ago, but our roots in this land go back even farther. Seated tastings on our covered patio allow you to take in the views while appreciating the wines. Our experienced staff provide an exceptional experience, and we love to answer your questions about our wines or the region. Winter's Hill Estate is located in the heart of the Dundee Hills and includes extensive natural areas of forest and meadows, as well as a Native Oak Savannah restoration site and birding trail.

Founded in 1989 by Ken and Grace Evenstad, Domaine Serene set out to craft world-class Pinot Noir. After three decades, we are proud to share our award-winning sparkling wine, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from both our Oregon and Burgundy estate vineyards. Our tasting room offers a range of experiences for every guest. Whether you’re enjoying a glass or bottle of wine, or embarking on a more immersive journey, we have something for all. We have Sommelier-led flights that dive deep into our rich story, or opt for a self-guided tasting with a curated food pairing. Walk-ins are always welcome, or you can make a reservation for more expanded experiences. Whether you wish to relax or learn, Domaine Serene is your premier destination for creating unique, unforgettable moments.
 
To explore our experiences or book online, visit: www.domaineserene.com/visit-us
Or contact us at (503) 864-4600 or hospitality@domaineserene.com

Experience Durant Vineyard’s estate-grown and crafted wines from our beautiful Dundee Hills Wine Tasting Room at Red Ridge Farms. Perched above the dramatic Willamette Valley, the Durant Vineyards Tasting Room features incredible views of Mt. Hood, Mt. Jefferson, and miles of diverse agricultural land. Enjoy seasonal bites from our talented Culinary Directors, expertly crafted to pair with our wines. Whether you’re a wine enthusiast or a casual taster, we provide a welcoming atmosphere to immerse yourself in the beauty of our surroundings and the quality of our wines. 

Reservations are recommended but not required. To make a reservation, please call 503.864.2000 Ext. 1 or visit www.durantoregon.com. We look forward to welcoming you!

At Sokol Blosser Winery, we offer a multitude of options to help you plan your perfect wine tasting experience. Although we recommend making a reservation online or giving us a call to reserve your table, we do accommodate walk-ins. Our seated tastings allow us to customize your tasting experience and add on culinary options from butcher's boards with a selection of meats and cheeses to culinary delights from our estate chef. 
 
Farm & Forage, our acclaimed food and wine pairing experience, is held Friday through Sunday at 1pm and requires a reservation. We host a Happy Hour in our Wine Bar from 5-7pm each Friday with a different theme every week. Every Saturday we offer a tour and wine tasting experience 10:30am. We’ll be firing up our pizza oven Memorial Weekend and running it seven days a week through the summer. Starting in June, you can join us for music and Frosé every Thursday for Live on the Lawn. Keep an eye out for Sunday vineyard hikes starting in July. 
 
More than 50 years ago, the Sokol Blosser family began growing grapes in the Dundee Hills of Oregon's Willamette Valley. Situated on a certified organic 88-acre vineyard in the Dundee Hills, with a new second 22-acre vineyard called Kalita in the Yamhill-Carlton AVA, and farming another 15 acres of vineyards in the Eola-Amity Hills, B Corp-certified Sokol Blosser Winery consistently captures the terroir of the region as expressed through the brilliance of its Estate fruit. One of the pioneering wineries of Oregon, Sokol Blosser is run by the Sokol Blosser family, who emphasize sustainability and good to the earth policies in every phase of the business.

Established in 1987, Domaine Drouhin Oregon is owned by the Drouhin Family of Burgundy. Fourth-generation winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin produces wines of distinction, balance, elegance, and longevity. The family’s principled approach to viticulture and winemaking and long experience with Pinot Noir and Chardonnay have earned an international reputation for excellence.

We are honored to invite you to experience wines made with French Soul from Oregon Soil. Our tasting room is open for indoor & outdoor tastings by appointment only from 10am to 3pm, daily. Please call us on (503)864-2700 with any questions.

Founded in 1990 by owner and winemaker Michael Mega, Nysa Vineyard was built on a foundation of passion, dedication and patience. Located in the heart of the Dundee Hills, it has grown into one of the region's most esteemed vineyard sites, supplying exceptional Pinot Noir grapes to top producers throughout the Willamette Valley. 

At Nysa, winemaking begins in the vineyard, where organic farming practices and a commitment to sustainability guide every step.The wines are thoughtfully crafted to achieve complexity, concentration and balance. Finesse, elegance and depth is obtained in the vineyard, but only years in the bottle allow these features to fully express in your glass. We've done that aging for you.

Wine tastings are offered at the winery, where you’ll enjoy our current releases of Sparkling, Chardonnay, and Pinot Noir, along with a selection of gracefully aged wines. Tastings are guided by our knowledgeable team and take place among the vines, with stunning views overlooking the valley.

More than 50 years ago, the Sokol Blosser family began growing grapes in the Dundee Hills of Oregon's Willamette Valley. Situated on a certified organic 88-acre vineyard in the Dundee Hills, with a new second 22-acre vineyard called Kalita in the Yamhill-Carlton AVA, and farming another 15 acres of vineyards in the Eola-Amity Hills, B Corp-certified Sokol Blosser Winery consistently captures the terroir of the region as expressed through the brilliance of its Estate fruit. One of the pioneering wineries of Oregon, Sokol Blosser is run by the Sokol Blosser family, who emphasize sustainability and good to the earth policies in every phase of the business.

Step into the legacy of one of Oregon's wine pioneers! Founded in 1971 by Cal Knudsen and Julia Lee, Knudsen Vineyards is a historic family-owned gem. Nestled in the heart of Dundee Hills, our 130-acre vineyard has a rich heritage of producing acclaimed wines.

Knudsen Vineyards is one of Oregon's oldest and largest family-owned vineyards. It is also the original site of Bonded Winery No. 52, the first commercial winery in the Dundee Hills. 

Knudsen Vineyard was a primary supplier of grapes to Knudsen Erath Winery from 1975 through 1986 and has been a leading supplier of fruit to Argyle Winery since 1987. 

In 2014, the Knudsen family returned to winemaking under its own label, and in 2020 they opened The Outlook at Knudsen Vineyards tasting room.

We produce Chardonnay, Rosé of Pinot noir, Pinot Noir and Sparkling wines.

Since its inception in 1993, Archery Summit has been devoted to crafting exceptional Pinot Noir from its more than 80 acres across five estate vineyards. Our philosophy is driven by a commitment to understanding the intricacies and unique personalities of each of our estate vineyards, allowing us to craft wines that vividly express their distinct places of origin. Over the past twenty-five years we have developed an international reputation for producing wines among the finest and most distinct of New World Pinot Noir. Our tasting room is open daily from 10am to 4pm by appointment. For more information on tastings and to reserve an experience, visit https://www.archerysummit.com/visit/ . We look forward to hosting you during your visit.

Come experience wine country like never before, with a taste of Burgundy in the heart of the Willamette Valley. Founded in 1989 by Ken and Grace Evenstad, Domaine Serene set out to craft world-class Pinot Noir. After three decades, we are proud to share our award-winning sparkling wine, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from both our Oregon and Burgundy estate vineyards. Our tasting room offers a range of experiences for every guest. Whether you’re enjoying a glass or bottle of wine, or embarking on a more immersive journey, we have something for all. We have Sommelier-led flights that dive deep into our rich story, or opt for a self-guided tasting with a curated food pairing. Walk-ins are always welcome, or you can make a reservation for more expanded experiences. Whether you wish to relax or learn, Domaine Serene is your premier destination for creating unique, unforgettable moments.
 
To explore our experiences or book online, visit: www.domaineserene.com/visit-us
Or contact us at (503) 864-4600 or hospitality@domaineserene.com

properties.Owner3: BOLKOVATZ JOSEPH F & LINDA J TRUSTEES OF

properties.winery_description: Eric Lemelson planted 3/4 of an acre of Pinot noir on a hillside farm east of Newberg in 1995. He enjoyed viticulture so much that within three years he planted more than 30 additional acres near Carlton and began to design the present day winery. Today, Lemelson Vineyards owns and manages over 124 acres at five sites spanning three AVAs. Each of the five estate vineyards have been farmed organically since the time their vines were first planted. Notably, all five vineyards have been certified organic by Oregon Tilth each year since October of 2004. The gravity-flow winery reflects a strong commitment to sustainability and a no compromises approach to quality winemaking.

Wetzel Estate Winery was established in 1990. The winery and vineyard are nestled in Oregon’s scenic Willamette Valley.

Take a breathtaking-scenic drive through the rolling hills of the mid-Willamette Valley.  Our tasting room is located on the top of Butler Hill with amazing views of the Valley just due East.  On clear days you can see Mt. St. Helen's to the North and Mt. Jefferson to the East.

Enjoy indoor, sit down, private or patio tastings from a vast selection of affordably priced wines.

Order a champagne cocktail and charcuterie board and take in the sights.  

Make a day of it and visit various attractions close by.  Golf, museums and the Oregon coast await!

Wine produced at the Estate benefit from a nearly perfect location. The Van Duzer Corridor A.V.A. (American Viticulture Area), is an optimal location benefitting from the maritime air currents which provide cooler temperatures, allowing for longer fruit hang time and development of the signature flavor profiles. In addition, this region is comprised of well-drained sedimentary soil with high mineral content, a result of the Missoula floods from thousands of years ago. The vineyard is L.I.V.E. Certified, one of the most stringent sustainability standards for vineyards of the Pacific Northwest.

The brands have widespread domestic and international distribution. Varietals produced include Pinot Noir, Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc, Riesling, Gewurztraminer, Marechal Foch, Chardonnay, and some limited-production specialty products including Sparkling Wines, a Ruby Port, Vin de Glace and Northwest Comfort the Wetzel family’s proprietary Gluhwein recipe.

Prince Hill Vineyards, located in the renowned Dundee Hills AVA of Willamette Valley, personifies a dedication to craftsmanship and stewardship. This journey expresses the exceptional terroir of the region and embraces one of the most storied vineyards in Oregon winemaking history. Since 2017, the Duncan family has been the trusted stewards of this acclaimed site, sustainably farming Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.

properties.Owner3: MULLEN GERALD P & KATHLEEN A TRUSTEES OF
BERGSTROM JOHN O & KAREN TRUSTEES FOR
properties.winery_description:
Since 1999, my family and team have been working towards our vision of stewarding naturally and responsibly farmed, estate vineyard terroirs that best represent the diverse appellations of our world-class Willamette Valley. Our Pinot Noirs and Chardonnays are a three-dimensional vinous tour of our beautiful state’s ancient volcanic and marine sedimentary hillsides.

All of us here at Bergström Wines are humbled by, and thankful for, the unwavering support of our community and clients. We give thanks to those who continue to make our labor of love possible by supporting our wines.

All tastings and wine pickups are held at our family home in the Dundee Hills. For information regarding reservations please email us at reservations@bergstromwines.com

Thank you once again for your support of our wines and our family and the Oregon winegrowing community!

Josh Bergström

Founded in 1987, Lange Estate is located in the heart of the Dundee Hills. Since the winery’s inception, the Lange family has focused on producing elegant, meticulously crafted examples of pinot noir, chardonnay, and pinot gris. Now spanning 60 acres, the property boasts a state-of-the-art winery and hospitality center. The tasting room features stunning panoramic views of vineyards, densely forested rolling valleys, and the Cascade Mountain Range. This destination winery has grown over the past thirty-five years to become an integral part of the renowned Willamette Valley appellation.

A Study in Willamette Valley Terroir. 'Single Vineyard' Pinot Noir and 'German Origin' Cool-Climate Whites that showcase the distinct and dramatic flavors of the Valley's diverse Appellations. Enjoy sweeping views of the Dundee Hills and Willamette Valley from our garden patio as you taste directly with Winegrowers/Owners, Tom & France and their amazing Bernese Mountain Dog, Four.  This is a micro-boutique, passion project from Burgundy Trained, UC Davis Educated Veteran Winegrower Tom Fitzpatrick.

Black Walnut Inn & Vineyard welcomes guests to an elevated experience in the heart of Oregon wine country. Our well-appointed nine-room villa is set above 100 acres of pinot noir and chardonnay vines, forests of Douglas Fir, and the Red Hills of Dundee. From our perch at an elevation of 950 feet, a spectacular view unfolds. Surrounding vineyards rest beneath an expansive horizon commanded by Mount Hood and Mount Jefferson. Visitors are afforded luxe accommodations and unmatched access to Oregon’s famed Willamette Valley.

Black Walnut Inn & Vineyard welcomes guests to an elevated experience in the heart of Oregon wine country. Our well-appointed nine-room villa is set above 100 acres of pinot noir and chardonnay vines, forests of Douglas Fir, and the Red Hills of Dundee. From our perch at an elevation of 950 feet, a spectacular view unfolds. Surrounding vineyards rest beneath an expansive horizon commanded by Mount Hood and Mount Jefferson. Visitors are afforded luxe accommodations and unmatched access to Oregon’s famed Willamette Valley.

Eric Lemelson planted 3/4 of an acre of Pinot noir on a hillside farm east of Newberg in 1995. He enjoyed viticulture so much that within three years he planted more than 30 additional acres near Carlton and began to design the present day winery. Today, Lemelson Vineyards owns and manages over 124 acres at five sites spanning three AVAs. Each of the five estate vineyards have been farmed organically since the time their vines were first planted. Notably, all five vineyards have been certified organic by Oregon Tilth each year since October of 2004. The gravity-flow winery reflects a strong commitment to sustainability and a no compromises approach to quality winemaking.

Torii Mor crafts small amounts of premium quality Pinot noir, Pinot gris, Pinot Blanc, and Chardonnay. Our devotion to quality begins in the vineyard and continues in the cellar, allowing us to create wine in a classic Burgundian fashion. Experience the wines and take in the beautiful Japanese garden with a breathtaking view of LIVE Certified Olson Estate Vineyard and the Willamette Valley. LEED Gold Certified.

Founded in 1987, Lange Estate is located in the heart of the Dundee Hills. Since the winery’s inception, the Lange family has focused on producing elegant, meticulously crafted examples of pinot noir, chardonnay, and pinot gris. Now spanning 60 acres, the property boasts a state-of-the-art winery and hospitality center. The tasting room features stunning panoramic views of vineyards, densely forested rolling valleys, and the Cascade Mountain Range. This destination winery has grown over the past thirty-five years to become an integral part of the renowned Willamette Valley appellation.

Step into the legacy of one of Oregon's wine pioneers! Founded in 1971 by Cal Knudsen and Julia Lee, Knudsen Vineyards is a historic family-owned gem. Nestled in the heart of Dundee Hills, our 130-acre vineyard has a rich heritage of producing acclaimed wines.

Knudsen Vineyards is one of Oregon's oldest and largest family-owned vineyards. It is also the original site of Bonded Winery No. 52, the first commercial winery in the Dundee Hills. 

Knudsen Vineyard was a primary supplier of grapes to Knudsen Erath Winery from 1975 through 1986 and has been a leading supplier of fruit to Argyle Winery since 1987. 

In 2014, the Knudsen family returned to winemaking under its own label, and in 2020 they opened The Outlook at Knudsen Vineyards tasting room.

We produce Chardonnay, Rosé of Pinot noir, Pinot Noir and Sparkling wines.

Our Gold LEED-Certified tasting room and 3 level gravity flow winery is the perfect place to explore the diversity and complexity of the Willamette Valley. With eighteen various sedimentary and volcanic soil types, our uniquely situated estate vineyard has earned a reputation for producing highly rated wines with exceptional balance and character. 'Diverse Soils, Complex Wines'

Come experience wine country like never before, with a taste of Burgundy in the heart of the Willamette Valley. Founded in 1989 by Ken and Grace Evenstad, Domaine Serene set out to craft world-class Pinot Noir. After three decades, we are proud to share our award-winning sparkling wine, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from both our Oregon and Burgundy estate vineyards. Our tasting room offers a range of experiences for every guest. Whether you’re enjoying a glass or bottle of wine, or embarking on a more immersive journey, we have something for all. We have Sommelier-led flights that dive deep into our rich story, or opt for a self-guided tasting with a curated food pairing. Walk-ins are always welcome, or you can make a reservation for more expanded experiences. Whether you wish to relax or learn, Domaine Serene is your premier destination for creating unique, unforgettable moments.
 
To explore our experiences or book online, visit: www.domaineserene.com/visit-us
Or contact us at (503) 864-4600 or hospitality@domaineserene.com

Perched upon a windy knoll 2 miles down a dirt road in Dallas, Oregon (no not Texas … this is Oregon) about an hour away from just about anywhere, you’ll find Van Duzer Vineyards. Here, 82 acres of vines lie in the direct path of cooling marine winds that rush through a deep gap in the Coast Range—its namesake Van Duzer Corridor. In this wild, beautiful, out of the way place —where the winds blow just right — you'll find our meticulously crafted wines. We're a bit off the beaten path, but we think that's where the best adventures are found.

We specialize in balanced and lively site-driven wines that reflect the unique impact of the wind and the marine soils. As the oldest winery in the AVA, Van Duzer Vineyards has naturally evolved into the defining producer of cool-climate Pinot Noir in the Van Duzer Corridor.

In this wild, beautiful, out of the way place —where the winds blow just right — you'll find our meticulously crafted wines. We're a bit off the beaten path, but we think that's where the best adventures are found.

We specialize in balanced and lively site-driven wines that reflect the unique impact of the wind and the marine soils. As the oldest winery in the AVA, Van Duzer Vineyards has naturally evolved into the defining producer of cool-climate Pinot Noir in the Van Duzer Corridor.

properties.winery_description: Native Flora is a small, secluded vineyard and winery hidden high in the famed Dundee Hills. Founded in 2005, we strive to produce premium estate wines in limited quantities for people who enjoy great wine, a good laugh, natural beauty and a more personal wine experience.Surrounded on three sides by forest and open sky on the fourth, the physical site is a haven from bustle, and a setting for stunning vistas. There is an inherent sense of tranquility filled with plenty of purpose. Visitors readily agree we truly are a sanctum - a private place where one is free from intrusion.Despite the inherent exclusivity of our diminutive size, we remain down to earth, hospitable, and enthusiastic about sharing our chosen path in life. Our aim is to enlist you not just as a customer, but as a new friend and fellow enthusiast of good wines and places.Our winery, tasting areas and home are all rolled into one surprising structure and endeavor. When you walk into our tasting room you have literally walked into our life. Welcome.-Scott & Denise Flora
